Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Swift Relocations rate increase notice using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "Greg Pallone" or "pricing update" or "rate increase"). Find Greg Pallone's email titled "Pricing Update Effective Immediately, DOT Documentation Surcharge" announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings, plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment. Both effective immediately as of April 11, 2026. Read full email using get_email_by_id for complete details including the rationale (extra administrative load from retroactive compliance paperwork).
OE 2: Search emails for Heartland Movers rate increase notice using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "rate adjustment" or "formal notice"). Find Jake Loomis's email titled "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review" announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers (linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, accessorial) effective July 1, 2026, for all MoveOps-managed relocations booked for Q3 and beyond. The email explicitly ties the rate increase to the unresolved Q1 billing dispute (invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042), stating Heartland leadership will not finalize new contract terms until the dispute is resolved. Read full email using get_email_by_id.
OE 3: Search Slack for internal vendor rate discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "vendor rate" or "vendor pricing" or "rate increase" or "vendor strategy"). Find multiple relevant messages: (a) Elena Rostova's directive in #executive (C004) ordering Marcus to deliver a vendor strategy memo by end of week, noting both vendors raising rates simultaneously and listing options (negotiate individually, replace one, split volume); (b) Marcus Thorne's #finance (C005) thread taking point on Elena's memo, noting Swift already pushed 15% live with $500 DOT surcharge, Heartland using the unresolved Q1 dispute as leverage, Lakeline as a limited backup option, and requesting client exposure input from David by 1 PM PT. Read full thread context using conversations_replies.
OE 4: Search Airtable for active Vectral Systems relocations using airtable_list_bases to find appMoveOpsOps001, then airtable_list_tables to find tblRelocations01, then airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: "Vectral Systems"). Find 5 active "In Progress" relocations: (1) Daniel Reeves, Seattle → San Diego, coordinator Suki Patel, premium package; (2) Maya Johansson, Portland → San Diego, coordinator Suki Patel, premium package; (3) Priya Suresh, Portland → Denver, coordinator Javier Morales, ESA German Shepherd requiring pet-friendly housing; (4) Terrence Nakamura, Austin → San Francisco, coordinator Suki Patel, ADA wheelchair user, SPLIT SHIPMENT: Heartland Movers (~6,000 lbs household) + Swift Relocations (~3,200 lbs motorized wheelchair + accessible equipment); (5) Camille Oduya, Boston → Seattle, coordinator Suki Patel, tight 2-day timeline with hard April 12 pickup.
OE 5: Search Airtable for active BrightLoop Analytics relocations using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: "BrightLoop"). Find 4 active "In Progress" relocations: (1) Jordan Whitfield, Minneapolis → Denver, coordinator Javier Morales, standard package; (2) Preethi Nair, Atlanta → Raleigh, coordinator Blessing Okafor, pet-friendly (cat); (3) Simone Richter, Chicago → Boston, coordinator Suki Patel, RUSH 5-day turnaround with $750 rush surcharge, Swift Relocations; (4) Marcus Webb, Atlanta → Boston, coordinator Suki Patel, Swift Relocations for household goods plus Road Runner Auto Transport for 2019 Honda Civic vehicle shipping.
OE 6: Search emails and Linear for vendor assignments on specific moves using search_emails (query: "Daniel Reeves" or "Maya Johansson" or "San Diego") and linear_list_issues (query: "Reeves" or "Johansson" or "Vectral"). Determine which vendor handles each Vectral and BrightLoop move. Key findings from emails, Airtable special requirements, and Linear ticket descriptions: Terrence Nakamura is the critical split-vendor case (Heartland + Swift); Simone Richter and Marcus Webb use Swift; Jordan Ekwueme's Linear ticket (#324) names Swift Relocations; Kevin Tran's completed move used Swift ($2,885 quote). For Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson (premium San Diego moves), vendor assignment is not explicitly documented in Airtable but they are long-distance West Coast moves typical of Swift Relocations.
OE 7: Search CRM for Vectral Systems deal data using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") to find the company record, then crm_search_deals (company_id for Vectral) to find all deals. Expected findings: deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk at stage "at_risk", the Q3 renewal negotiation with Rachel Whitfield (15% demand, 10% counter-offer pending board decision April 23); deal_vectral_april_2026 at $15,900, stage "closedwon" (Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya); deal_vectral_q2 at $11,000 (Kevin Tran + Danielle Osei). Check engagement notes using crm_list_engagements for negotiation history.
OE 8: Search CRM for BrightLoop Analytics deal data using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop") then crm_search_deals. Expected findings: deal_brightloop_q2 at $7,000, stage "qualifiedtobuy" (Jordan Ekwueme); deal_brightloop_apr2026 at $11,350, stage "closedwon" (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb with rush surcharge and vehicle shipping).
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills related to Heartland and Swift using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria for vendor Heartland) and quickbooks_search_bills (criteria for vendor Swift). Find the Heartland Q1 bill (bill_heartland_q1_2026) at $12,800 for 8 moves and the hold record (bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold) reflecting the disputed status with $4,800 contested. Find Swift vendor record (vendor_swift) with balance $11,010. Check whether any recent bills reflect the new 15% rate.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for Vectral and BrightLoop client invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for customer Vectral or BrightLoop). Find Vectral invoices: inv_vectral_tran ($5,397 for Kevin Tran), invoice 1002 ($16,000 for Daniel Reeves + Maya Johansson premium packages), inv_vectral_april_2026 ($15,900 for Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya). Find BrightLoop invoices: invoice 1005 ($10,000 for Whitfield + Nair), invoice 1008 ($11,350 for Richter + Webb including $750 rush surcharge and $1,100 vehicle shipping). These reveal the current pricing baseline against which rate increases would be measured.
OE 11: Search emails for Lakeline backup vendor conversation using search_emails (query: "Lakeline" or "Sandra Walsh" or "backup vendor"). Find Chloe Vance's outreach to Sandra Walsh at Lakeline and Sandra's reply. Key finding: Lakeline covers Midwest and Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX) with rates roughly 10% below Heartland, but CANNOT serve as a national replacement, no West Coast lanes, no international, limited overflow capacity. This means Lakeline cannot help with BrightLoop's Boston-bound moves (Simone Richter from Chicago, Marcus Webb from Atlanta) or Vectral's San Francisco/San Diego/Seattle lanes, but could potentially cover Jordan Whitfield's Minneapolis → Denver or Preethi Nair's Atlanta → Raleigh.
OE 12: Search emails for the Heartland Q1 billing dispute using search_emails (query: "Heartland dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or "invoice dispute"). Find Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis contesting $4,800 of the $12,800 Q1 invoice, 3 invalid charges: (1) Lily Marchetti (Canopy Health) cancelled February 10; (2) Danielle Osei (Vectral Systems) cancelled February 13; (3) Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack Energy) handled by Swift, not Heartland. Also find Chloe Vance's internal analysis email identifying the same 3 disputed moves. MoveOps position: only 5 of 8 moves are valid ($8,000 corrected total). Payment hold placed in QuickBooks.
OE 13: Search Linear for existing vendor-related tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor" or "Heartland" or "rate"). Find issue_heartland_q1_recon (Linear #342: "Vendor invoice reconciliation, Heartland Movers Q1 2026", team_relocations, P2, state: in_progress, assigned to Chloe Vance). Confirm NO existing Linear ticket specifically tracks the client-side impact of the dual vendor rate increases, a new ticket is needed.
OE 14: Look up David Chen's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 15: Look up Mina Hashimoto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 16: Update CRM with Vectral vendor risk exposure using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body covering: Swift 15% base rate increase effective immediately affects Terrence Nakamura's split shipment Swift portion and potentially Daniel Reeves/Maya Johansson; Heartland 20% increase effective July 1 affects Nakamura's household shipment if Q3 timeline; total Vectral vendor exposure per move; critical context that this overlaps with the at-risk Q3 renewal negotiation, any price pass-through risks losing Rachel Whitfield; Lakeline backup does not cover Vectral's West Coast lanes). Associate with Vectral company_id.
OE 17: Update CRM with BrightLoop vendor risk exposure using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body covering: Swift 15% increase + $500 DOT surcharge affects Simone Richter and Marcus Webb (both Swift-handled, moves potentially already billed at new rates since Swift increase was effective April 11); Heartland 20% July 1 increase relevant for future May expansion moves; Lakeline cannot cover Boston lanes but may cover Jordan Whitfield Minneapolis → Denver; Tessa already sensitive about service quality, cost surprises risky). Associate with BrightLoop company_id.
OE 18: Send email to David Chen using send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: [david.chen@moveops.com]) with subject referencing vendor rate exposure for Mina's accounts and content covering: (a) Swift rate changes, 15% base increase effective April 11 plus $500 DOT surcharge per shipment; (b) Heartland rate changes, 20% increase effective July 1, tied to unresolved Q1 dispute ($4,800 contested on $12,800 invoice); (c) Vectral exposure, 5 active relocations, Terrence Nakamura directly affected on both vendors (split shipment), renewal negotiation at risk if costs passed through; (d) BrightLoop exposure, 4 active relocations, Simone Richter and Marcus Webb moves may already be affected by Swift's immediate increase, 6 additional May moves incoming; (e) Lakeline backup limitations, Midwest/Southeast only, does not cover Boston or West Coast lanes needed by both clients; (f) Recommendation for the vendor strategy memo Elena ordered.
OE 19: Post summary in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002, payload: summary of vendor rate increase impact on Mina's Vectral and BrightLoop accounts, Swift 15% immediate + $500 DOT surcharge, Heartland 20% July 1, which moves are exposed, Lakeline backup limitations, flag that Vectral renewal and BrightLoop expansion are both at risk from cost increases).
OE 20: Create Linear ticket for client-side vendor impact tracking using linear_create_issue (team: team_relocations, title: "Vendor Rate Increase, Client Impact Assessment (Vectral + BrightLoop)", description covering: dual vendor rate increases, per-account exposure for Vectral (5 active moves, split-vendor Nakamura, renewal at risk) and BrightLoop (4 active + 6 May moves, service recovery in progress), Lakeline backup gap analysis, link to Elena's vendor strategy memo request, priority: P2 or P1).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Swift Relocations rate increase notice using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "Greg Pallone" or "pricing update" or "rate increase"). Find Greg Pallone's email titled "Pricing Update Effective Immediately, DOT Documentation Surcharge" announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings, plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment. Both effective immediately as of April 11, 2026. Read full email using get_email_by_id for complete details including the rationale (extra administrative load from retroactive compliance paperwork).
OE 2: Search emails for Heartland Movers rate increase notice using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "rate adjustment" or "formal notice"). Find Jake Loomis's email titled "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review" announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers (linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, accessorial) effective July 1, 2026, for all MoveOps-managed relocations booked for Q3 and beyond. The email explicitly ties the rate increase to the unresolved Q1 billing dispute (invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042), stating Heartland leadership will not finalize new contract terms until the dispute is resolved. Read full email using get_email_by_id.
OE 3: Search Slack for internal vendor rate discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "vendor rate" or "vendor pricing" or "rate increase" or "vendor strategy"). Find multiple relevant messages: (a) Elena Rostova's directive in #executive (C004) ordering Marcus to deliver a vendor strategy memo by end of week, noting both vendors raising rates simultaneously and listing options (negotiate individually, replace one, split volume); (b) Marcus Thorne's #finance (C005) thread taking point on Elena's memo, noting Swift already pushed 15% live with $500 DOT surcharge, Heartland using the unresolved Q1 dispute as leverage, Lakeline as a limited backup option, and requesting client exposure input from David by 1 PM PT. Read full thread context using conversations_replies.
OE 4: Search Airtable for active Vectral Systems relocations using airtable_list_bases to find appMoveOpsOps001, then airtable_list_tables to find tblRelocations01, then airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: "Vectral Systems"). Find 5 active "In Progress" relocations: (1) Daniel Reeves, Seattle → San Diego, coordinator Suki Patel, premium package; (2) Maya Johansson, Portland → San Diego, coordinator Suki Patel, premium package; (3) Priya Suresh, Portland → Denver, coordinator Javier Morales, ESA German Shepherd requiring pet-friendly housing; (4) Terrence Nakamura, Austin → San Francisco, coordinator Suki Patel, ADA wheelchair user, SPLIT SHIPMENT: Heartland Movers (~6,000 lbs household) + Swift Relocations (~3,200 lbs motorized wheelchair + accessible equipment); (5) Camille Oduya, Boston → Seattle, coordinator Suki Patel, tight 2-day timeline with hard April 12 pickup.
OE 5: Search Airtable for active BrightLoop Analytics relocations using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: "BrightLoop"). Find 4 active "In Progress" relocations: (1) Jordan Whitfield, Minneapolis → Denver, coordinator Javier Morales, standard package; (2) Preethi Nair, Atlanta → Raleigh, coordinator Blessing Okafor, pet-friendly (cat); (3) Simone Richter, Chicago → Boston, coordinator Suki Patel, RUSH 5-day turnaround with $750 rush surcharge, Swift Relocations; (4) Marcus Webb, Atlanta → Boston, coordinator Suki Patel, Swift Relocations for household goods plus Road Runner Auto Transport for 2019 Honda Civic vehicle shipping.
OE 6: Search emails and Linear for vendor assignments on specific moves using search_emails (query: "Daniel Reeves" or "Maya Johansson" or "San Diego") and linear_list_issues (query: "Reeves" or "Johansson" or "Vectral"). Determine which vendor handles each Vectral and BrightLoop move. Key findings from emails, Airtable special requirements, and Linear ticket descriptions: Terrence Nakamura is the critical split-vendor case (Heartland + Swift); Simone Richter and Marcus Webb use Swift; Jordan Ekwueme's Linear ticket (#324) names Swift Relocations; Kevin Tran's completed move used Swift ($2,885 quote). For Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson (premium San Diego moves), vendor assignment is not explicitly documented in Airtable but they are long-distance West Coast moves typical of Swift Relocations.
OE 7: Search CRM for Vectral Systems deal data using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") to find the company record, then crm_search_deals (company_id for Vectral) to find all deals. Expected findings: deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk at stage "at_risk", the Q3 renewal negotiation with Rachel Whitfield (15% demand, 10% counter-offer pending board decision April 23); deal_vectral_april_2026 at $15,900, stage "closedwon" (Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya); deal_vectral_q2 at $11,000 (Kevin Tran + Danielle Osei). OE 8: Search CRM for BrightLoop Analytics deal data using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop") then crm_search_deals. OE 10: Search QuickBooks for Vectral and BrightLoop client invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for customer Vectral or BrightLoop). Find Vectral invoices: inv_vectral_tran ($5,397 for Kevin Tran), invoice 1002 ($16,000 for Daniel Reeves + Maya Johansson premium packages), inv_vectral_april_2026 ($15,900 for Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya). OE 11: Search emails for Lakeline backup vendor conversation using search_emails (query: "Lakeline" or "Sandra Walsh" or "backup vendor"). Find Chloe Vance's outreach to Sandra Walsh at Lakeline and Sandra's reply. Key finding: Lakeline covers Midwest and Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX) with rates roughly 10% below Heartland, but CANNOT serve as a national replacement, no West Coast lanes, no international, limited overflow capacity. This means Lakeline cannot help with BrightLoop's Boston-bound moves (Simone Richter from Chicago, Marcus Webb from Atlanta) or Vectral's San Francisco/San Diego/Seattle lanes, but could potentially cover Jordan Whitfield's Minneapolis → Denver or Preethi Nair's Atlanta → Raleigh.
OE 12: Search emails for the Heartland Q1 billing dispute using search_emails (query: "Heartland dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or "invoice dispute"). Find Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis contesting $4,800 of the $12,800 Q1 invoice, 3 invalid charges: (1) Lily Marchetti (Canopy Health) cancelled February 10; (2) Danielle Osei (Vectral Systems) cancelled February 13; (3) Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack Energy) handled by Swift, not Heartland. Also find Chloe Vance's internal analysis email identifying the same 3 disputed moves. MoveOps position: only 5 of 8 moves are valid ($8,000 corrected total). Payment hold placed in QuickBooks.
OE 13: Search Linear for existing vendor-related tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor" or "Heartland" or "rate"). OE 17: Update CRM with BrightLoop vendor risk exposure using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body covering: Swift 15% increase + $500 DOT surcharge affects Simone Richter and Marcus Webb (both Swift-handled, moves potentially already billed at new rates since Swift increase was effective April 11); Heartland 20% July 1 increase relevant for future May expansion moves; Lakeline cannot cover Boston lanes but may cover Jordan Whitfield Minneapolis → Denver; Tessa already sensitive about service quality, cost surprises risky). Associate with BrightLoop company_id.
OE 18: Send email to David Chen using send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: [david.chen@moveops.com]) with subject referencing vendor rate exposure for Mina's accounts and content covering: (a) Swift rate changes, 15% base increase effective April 11 plus $500 DOT surcharge per shipment; (b) Heartland rate changes, 20% increase effective July 1, tied to unresolved Q1 dispute ($4,800 contested on $12,800 invoice); (c) Vectral exposure, 5 active relocations, Terrence Nakamura directly affected on both vendors (split shipment), renewal negotiation at risk if costs passed through; (d) BrightLoop exposure, 4 active relocations, Simone Richter and Marcus Webb moves may already be affected by Swift's immediate increase, 6 additional May moves incoming; (e) Lakeline backup limitations, Midwest/Southeast only, does not cover Boston or West Coast lanes needed by both clients; (f) Recommendation for the vendor strategy memo Elena ordered.

OE 20: Create Linear ticket for client-side vendor impact tracking using linear_create_issue (team: team_relocations, title: "Vendor Rate Increase, Client Impact Assessment (Vectral + BrightLoop)", description covering: dual vendor rate increases, per-account exposure for Vectral (5 active moves, split-vendor Nakamura, renewal at risk) and BrightLoop (4 active + 6 May moves, service recovery in progress), Lakeline backup gap analysis, link to Elena's vendor strategy memo request, priority: P2 or P1).
